title: 10 best herbal tinctures for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for psoriasis are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are taken orally or applied topically to alleviate psoriasis symptoms. These tinctures offer a natural approach to manage psoriasis, targeting inflammation, itching, and skin cell growth. Some popular herbal tinctures include milk thistle, burdock root, and Oregon grape root. For instance, milk thistle supports liver function, which aids in eliminating toxins that can exacerbate psoriasis. By incorporating these tinctures into a treatment plan, individuals often experience reduced inflammation, fewer skin lesions, and enhanced quality of life, enabling them to better manage this chronic skin condition.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and reduce the redness and irritation associated with psoriasis. Its moisturizing and hydrating effects help to alleviate dryness and flaking of the skin, while its antioxidant properties protect the skin from further damage. The polysaccharides found in aloe vera also help to stimulate the growth of new skin cells, promoting the healing and regeneration of affected areas. Furthermore, aloe vera has been shown to inhibit the production of certain chemicals in the body that contribute to the development of psoriasis, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing this skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tincture reduces inflammation and soothes irritation in psoriasis lesions, thanks to the presence of active compounds like salicylic acid, bradykinase, and various anthraquinones.
Moisturizing: Aloe vera tincture hydrates and softens the skin, promoting healing and reducing redness and scaling in psoriasis, due to the polysaccharide content, particularly acemannan.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera tincture protects skin cells from oxidative stress and damage, supporting skin regeneration and reducing the appearance of psoriasis lesions, attributed to the presence of vitamins A, C, and E, and other antioxidant compounds.
Antimicrobial: Aloe vera tincture helps prevent bacterial and fungal infections in psoriasis-affected skin, thanks to the presence of various antimicrobial agents like lupeol, phenols, and sulfur.

Immunomodulatory: Aloe vera tincture regulates the immune response, which is crucial for managing psoriasis symptoms, as the condition is characterized by an overactive immune system. This is due to the presence of glycoproteins, lectins, and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a clear gel that is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making them useful for soothing the red, itchy, and scaly skin associated with psoriasis.

Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a slimy tissue that is rich in polysaccharides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for reducing the inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis.

Seeds: The seeds of the aloe vera plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage and reduce the oxidative stress that can contribute to the development of psoriasis.

Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant contain nectar that is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for reducing the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 large leaves from an Aloe vera plant, carefully removing the green outer skin to extract the inner clear gel.
Measure 1 cup of fresh Aloe vera gel and combine it with 2 cups of a menstruum (such as grain alcohol or apple cider vinegar) in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days to ensure even extraction.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid and discarding the solid plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dry place for up to a year and applying it as needed to affected psoriasis areas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin condition. Burdock is known to detoxify the blood, eliminate toxins, and promote healthy blood circulation, which can help reduce the inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe the skin, reduce itching, and promote the healing of psoriasis lesions. Burdock tincture can also help regulate the immune system, which is often overactive in people with psoriasis, leading to the overproduction of skin cells. By addressing these underlying causes, burdock tincture can provide relief and improve the overall health of individuals with psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce psoriasis-related redness, swelling, and discomfort due to the presence of essential fatty acids and tannins.
Antioxidant: The strong antioxidant effects of burdock tincture help protect skin cells from damage, which is crucial for managing psoriasis symptoms, as it contains phenolic acids and quercetin.
Blood purification: Burdock tincture's ability to cleanse the blood and eliminate toxins supports overall skin health and reduces psoriasis symptoms, thanks to its content of arctigen and lignans.

Immune-modulating: Burdock tincture can help regulate immune responses, which is vital for controlling psoriasis flare-ups and reducing skin cell overproduction, due to its content of polysaccharides and arctiin.
Antimicrobial: Burdock tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent skin infections and maintain skin health, which is important for psoriasis management, as it contains volatile oils and resins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties.
Leaves: Burdock leaves, rich in antioxidants, help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: The seeds of burdock contain essential fatty acids and have been found to help alleviate psoriasis symptoms, such as itching and dryness.
Flowers: Burdock flowers are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, making them a popular addition to teas for psoriasis relief.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried burdock root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the burdock root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use as needed for psoriasis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the skin condition. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help alleviate the symptoms of psoriasis. Furthermore, pot marigold tincture has been found to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Its soothing and calming effects on the skin make it an ideal natural remedy for individuals seeking relief from psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for psoriasis as it is a key feature of the condition, and is due to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenes.
Wound healing: The tincture can promote wound healing, beneficial for psoriasis as it often involves skin lesions and broken skin, and is attributed to the presence of the compound, oleanolic acid.
Antipruritic: Pot marigold tincture can alleviate itching, which is a common symptom of psoriasis, due to the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of the plant.
Antimicrobial: The tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections in the skin lesions associated with psoriasis, and is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.

Immune modulating: Pot marigold tincture can help regulate the immune system, which is overactive in psoriasis, contributing to the development and worsening of symptoms, and is due to the presence of the compound, lutein.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers help soothe irritated skin and reduce psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: Containing high levels of flavonoids, pot marigold leaves help alleviate inflammation and support skin health in psoriasis treatment.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, pot marigold seeds aid in reducing skin dryness and scaling associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar with approximately 2 cups of fresh flowers.
Submerge the flowers in 80-proof vodka, ensuring they are completely covered and leaving a 1-inch headspace in the jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent flowers.
Transfer the tincture to a sterilized glass storage bottle and store in a cool, dark place; apply topically as needed for psoriasis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can support skin health. The antioxidants in bell peppers, such as vitamin C and carotenoids, can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, which are both common factors in psoriasis. Additionally, bell peppers contain capsaicin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and itching associated with psoriasis. Furthermore, the vitamin E in bell peppers can help moisturize the skin, reducing dryness and flaking, which are common symptoms of psoriasis. Overall, the combination of these nutrients makes bell pepper tincture a valuable addition to a psoriasis treatment plan.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of antioxidants like vitamin C and carotenoids, can help reduce psoriasis-related inflammation and redness.
Improving immune function: The high vitamin C content in bell pepper tincture supports a healthy immune system, which is crucial for managing the autoimmune aspect of psoriasis.

Antioxidant-rich: The antioxidant-rich nature of bell pepper tincture, owing to its carotenoids and vitamin C, aids in neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, common in psoriasis patients.
Skin-soothing: Bell pepper tincture's skin-soothing properties, attributed to its vitamin E and carotenoids, can help alleviate itching, irritation, and dryness associated with psoriasis.
Pain-relieving: The pain-relieving effects of bell pepper tincture, resulting from its capsaicin content, can provide relief from the discomfort caused by psoriasis lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing psoriasis symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, they have antipruritic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate itching and redness associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and vitamins, they can help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Chop one cup of organic bell peppers and one cup of herbs known to treat psoriasis, such as calendula, chamomile, or cleavers.
Combine the chopped herbs and bell peppers with one quart of 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Securely cover the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to one year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis. The tincture form allows for better absorption of curcumin into the bloodstream, increasing its effectiveness. Additionally, turmeric has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Its antimicrobial properties may also help prevent skin infections that can occur in psoriasis lesions. Overall, turmeric tincture can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and improve skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to curcumin, help reduce psoriasis-related swelling, redness, and pain.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from curcumin, helps combat free radicals and alleviate oxidative stress in psoriasis-affected skin.
Immunomodulatory: Turmeric tincture's ability to modulate the immune system, thanks to curcumin and other curcuminoids, can help control the overactive immune response in psoriasis.
Wound-healing: Its wound-healing properties, attributed to curcumin and other compounds, aid in tissue repair and skin regeneration in psoriasis lesions.
Antipruritic: Turmeric tincture's antipruritic effects, resulting from curcumin, help alleviate itching, a common and distressing symptom of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the turmeric plant are used to make medicinal tea for psoriasis due to their high curcumin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for psoriasis due to their rich antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage and reduce inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for psoriasis due to their potential to inhibit the growth of skin cells, which can help reduce the scaling and redness associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: The seeds of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for psoriasis due to their high oil content, which can help moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid remains.
Store the finished turmeric tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and apply topically to affected psoriasis areas as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties can help reduce the redness, irritation, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, which can help combat the overproduction of skin cells that occurs in people with psoriasis. The herbal tincture can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected areas, providing relief and potentially slowing down the progression of the condition. Furthermore, the antimicrobial and antioxidant effects of eastern purple coneflower tincture can help protect the skin and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps reduce inflammation in psoriasis lesions, which is important because inflammation contributes to the development and worsening of psoriasis symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of chrysanthemulin, a sesquiterpene lactone with potent anti-inflammatory effects.

Immune-modulating: The tincture helps regulate the overactive immune response that leads to psoriasis symptoms. This is crucial for psoriasis management as the condition is characterized by an overactive immune system. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides, which have been shown to modulate immune function.

Antiproliferative: Eastern purple coneflower tincture inhibits the excessive skin cell growth and division that occur in psoriasis. This property is significant because it addresses one of the key features of psoriasis, the rapid accumulation of skin cells. The antiproliferative effects are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as chrysanthemulin and germacranolides.

Antioxidant: The tincture contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Antioxidants are essential for psoriasis treatment as they help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin. The antioxidant properties can be attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids in eastern purple coneflower.

Wound-healing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is important for managing psoriasis lesions. This property is valuable for psoriasis patients as it aids in the repair of damaged skin and reduces the risk of infection. The wound-healing effects are attributed to the presence of triterpenes, which have been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: Contain beneficial flavonoids, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for psoriasis relief.
Stems: Packed with soothing compounds, stems aid in reducing skin irritation and inflammation.
Buds: High in antioxidants, buds help protect skin cells and promote healing for psoriasis.
Seeds: Seeds provide essential oils that moisturize and nourish the skin, supporting psoriasis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves at peak bloom, ensuring they are dry and free of pests or diseases.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried herb into small pieces, then place them in a clean, dry glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Pour 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a clean, dry glass bottle or jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herbs. Discard the spent herbs.

Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, ingredients, date of preparation, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL, 2-3 times per day). Store the tincture in a cool, dark place, where it will last for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of silica and selenium, which are essential for maintaining healthy skin. These minerals support the production of collagen and elastin, two proteins that help keep skin strong and flexible. Additionally, horsetail tincture has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with psoriasis. The herb also promotes blood circulation, which can help improve the overall health and appearance of the skin. By providing these benefits, horsetail tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of psoriasis and support the skin's natural healing processes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from silica and selenium, helps protect skin cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by psoriasis.
Antimicrobial: Horsetail tincture's antimicrobial properties, owing to the compound equisetin, can help maintain skin health and prevent potential infections in psoriasis-prone areas.

Collagen synthesis promotion: The high silica content in horsetail tincture supports collagen synthesis, which is crucial for maintaining healthy skin and aiding in the healing process for psoriasis lesions.
Diuretic: Horsetail tincture's diuretic properties, attributed to the presence of saponins, can help eliminate toxins and reduce fluid retention, which can alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail's rhizomes are rich in silica and other minerals, making them useful in reducing inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe skin irritation and promote skin health in psoriasis.
Leaves: The leaves of horsetail are a great source of skin-nourishing nutrients, including vitamins and minerals, which can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried horsetail herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, discarding the spent herb.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage (typically 30-60 drops, 2-3 times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, chemicals that play a key role in the development of psoriasis. Additionally, St. John's wort has been found to have antioxidant effects, which can help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, a contributing factor in psoriasis. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of psoriasis, such as redness, itching, and scaling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for psoriasis as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and pain in affected areas. The active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antipruritic: St. John's wort tincture has an itch-relieving effect, which is crucial for psoriasis management as it helps minimize discomfort and scratching, preventing the worsening of lesions. Hyperforin is the primary active constituent responsible for this antipruritic effect.

Antioxidant: The tincture contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage, supporting the overall health of the skin and aiding in the healing process. Hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids are the primary antioxidant compounds found in St. John's wort.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties, which are important for psoriasis management as they help prevent infections in broken skin and reduce the risk of complications. The active constituents responsible for its antimicrobial properties include hyperforin and flavonoids.

Immune-modulating: St. John's wort tincture can help regulate the immune system, which is essential for psoriasis treatment as it addresses the overactive immune response that contributes to the development of psoriasis symptoms. Hypericin and hyperforin are the primary active constituents that contribute to its immune-modulating effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, the flowers of St. John's Wort are used to make medicinal tea for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Containing similar compounds as the flowers, St. John's Wort leaves are used in tea to help soothe irritated skin and alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Aerial Parts: Not included, as they are not typically used in the preparation of medicinal tea for psoriasis.
Stems: While stems contain some beneficial compounds, they are not primarily used in the tea preparation, as other parts have higher concentrations of active ingredients.
Roots: Not used, as they do not contain the desired levels of active compounds for treating psoriasis.
Rhizomes: Not used, as they are not a significant source of the beneficial compounds found in St. John's Wort.
Seeds: Not used, as they do not contain significant amounts of the active compounds required for treating psoriasis.
Buds: Not used, as they are not a primary source of the beneficial compounds in St. John's Wort.
Bark: Not used, as it is not a significant source of the beneficial compounds required for treating psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and dry, typically in mid-summer.
Chop or crush 2 ounces of the herb, placing it in a clean glass jar, then pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herbs to cover them completely.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid from the marc, and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Apply the St. John's Wort tincture topically to affected psoriasis areas, diluting it with a carrier oil if desired, and avoid exposure to sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creosote bush tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains active compounds like nordihydroguaiaretic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce the redness, irritation, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, creosote bush has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which may help prevent skin infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Furthermore, the tincture can help regulate skin cell growth, which is often dysregulated in individuals with psoriasis. By addressing these underlying factors, creosote bush tincture can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and improve overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Creosote bush tincture helps reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis, which is important because psoriasis is characterized by red, inflamed skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include nor-dihydroguiaretic acid and guaiaretic acid.
Antipruritic: It alleviates itching, a common symptom of psoriasis, by reducing histamine release. The compound primarily responsible for this property is guaiaconic acid.

Antimicrobial: Creosote bush tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping to prevent skin infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include guaiaconic acid, guaiaretic acid, and nordihydroguiaretic acid.
Antiproliferative: It slows down excessive skin cell growth and shedding, a key characteristic of psoriasis. The compound responsible for this property is guaiaconic acid.
Analgesic: Creosote bush tincture helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with psoriasis. The active constituents responsible for this property include guaiaconic acid and guaiaretic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Stems: May have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe skin affected by psoriasis.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that can help protect skin cells and reduce oxidative stress in psoriasis.
Bark: Has been used traditionally to treat skin conditions, including psoriasis, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest creosote bush leaves in late spring or early fall, choosing only healthy, mature leaves.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried creosote bush leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape tincture helps with psoriasis because it contains berberine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties can help reduce the redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, Oregon grape tincture may help regulate the immune system, which is believed to play a role in the development of psoriasis. The herb may also help improve skin cell turnover, reducing the buildup of skin cells that can lead to psoriasis lesions. Overall, Oregon grape tincture offers a natural and potentially effective option for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregon grape tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for psoriasis as it is a key factor in the development and worsening of symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties come from alkaloids such as berberine and hydrastine.

Antimicrobial: Oregon grape tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can be beneficial for psoriasis since some cases are associated with infections. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to berberine and other alkaloids.

Immune modulating: Oregon grape tincture can help regulate the immune system, which is often overactive in people with psoriasis. This property is important because psoriasis is an autoimmune condition. The immune modulating properties are due to the alkaloids, particularly berberine.

Antipruritic: Oregon grape tincture can alleviate itching, a common and distressing symptom of psoriasis. This property is important because itching can lead to scratching, which can further damage the skin and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. The antipruritic properties are attributed to the alkaloids, especially berberine.

Antioxidant: Oregon grape tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for psoriasis as oxidative stress can contribute to the development and progression of the condition. The antioxidant properties are due to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Oregon grape plant contain berberine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Oregon grape plant also contain berberine and have been used traditionally to treat skin conditions like psoriasis.
Leaves: The leaves of the Oregon grape plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe inflamed skin and reduce redness associated with psoriasis.
Bark: The bark of the Oregon grape plant contains alkaloids, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, potentially helpful in managing the overactive immune response in psoriasis.
Flowers: The flowers of the Oregon grape plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest Oregon grape root, removing bark with a knife, and letting it dry for 3-5 days.
Chop or grind 1 cup of dried Oregon grape root bark into small pieces, placing them in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or rum over the chopped bark, ensuring it is fully submerged, then seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle for up to a year. Take 30 drops, 3 times a day, as needed for psoriasis relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of ingredients known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Look for tinctures containing calendula, milk thistle, and burdock root. These herbs can help reduce redness, irritation, and scaling associated with psoriasis. For optimal results, consider taking the tincture orally and applying it topically to affected areas. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal tinctures include eczema and dermatitis. These skin conditions, like psoriasis, can cause redness, itching, and irritation. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from calendula, chamomile, or witch hazel, can help alleviate these symptoms. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making it effective in soothing irritated skin. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help reduce redness and fight infection. Witch hazel has astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and itching. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
